summary: Phase A refactor (referrals + leaderboard routers extracted from server.py into /app/backend/routers/) is behaviour-neutral. All 49 backend tests passed (1 expected skip for E2E interview-complete that needs a pro token). Smoke-checked refer/validate (empty/valid/bogus) and confirmed the untouched /api/leaderboard/student/me/ranks is still mounted (returned 403 for admin role as expected, not 404). Routers correctly import shared helpers (db, current_user, require_role, REFERRAL_REWARD, make_referral_code, referral_link_for, compute_tps, compute_wps, MASTER_SKILLS) from server.py and are registered via api.include_router() before app.include_router(api).

minor.issue: File listed in files_of_reference but does not exist on disk. Python 3 namespace packages mean imports still work, but adding an empty __init__.py is cleaner for explicit package semantics and IDE tooling.

action_items: (Optional) Create empty /app/backend/routers/__init__.py for explicit package declaration — currently works via namespace packages but PRD mentioned it as part of structure.

(Optional, unrelated to this refactor) Migrate FastAPI @app.on_event('startup'/'shutdown') in server.py to the lifespan event handler pattern — flagged by DeprecationWarning at server.py:3412, 3766, 4334.
critical_code_review_comments: server.py is 4336 lines — still well over the 700-line guideline. Phase A is a good start; recommend continuing extraction (auth, jobs, interviews, wallet) in subsequent phases.

routers/referrals.py and routers/leaderboard.py do `from server import ...` — this creates a circular-import shape that only works because the include_router() lives at the bottom of server.py. Document this constraint in a module docstring so future contributors don't move the include block higher.

routers/leaderboard.py:152 — `my_pro_stats` calls `leaderboard_pros(u)` directly. This re-runs the full pros aggregation per /me/stats call (O(N pros) + O(N jobs aggregate)). With >1000 pros + a `.to_list(None)` (no cap), this is a hot path. Consider extracting the WPS computation into a helper and only running the full board sort on demand.

success_rate.frontend: N/A — frontend testing skipped per request

seed_data_creation: Reused existing seed user with referral_code=USERECE1AD84 (seseendra@referme.io). Test suites create their own throw-away TEST_* users via signup+verify-otp.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Phase A modular-router refactor verified green. /api/refer/{validate,me,list} now live in /app/backend/routers/referrals.py; /api/leaderboard/{students,students/options,professionals,professional/me/stats} in /app/backend/routers/leaderboard.py. URLs and response shapes unchanged. The untouched /api/leaderboard/student/me/ranks remains in server.py. Subsequent refactor phases (auth, jobs, interviews, wallet) can use the same pattern: bottom-of-server include_router + `from server import ...` in router modules.
rca_of_issue: No bugs found. The refactor preserves all behaviour because (1) router modules import every shared helper named in the review request, (2) include_router happens after all helpers are defined in server.py, and (3) URLs are mounted on the same `api` APIRouter with prefix='/api' so external URLs are unchanged.
